raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should 'Check this' mean argument/evidence validity, writing polish, or novelty/citation fact-checking?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The artifact states a contribution and gap-like positioning that a general check would be expected to assess above the sentence level.","R1.2":"A general check centrally includes whether the methodological setup, results, and conclusions follow coherently.","R1.3":"The delegation plausibly asks whether the reported percentages, confidence intervals, and training result support the claims made.","R1.4":"The text makes a 'first calibration' contribution claim, so checking may centrally involve novelty relative to prior work.","R1.6":"A broad 'Check this' request commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified.","RX.2":"The artifact contains potentially strong claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.","RX.3":"The agent must not execute edits or perform tasks beyond projecting the delegation.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported downstream use.","RX.5":"The projection must remain traceable to the given delegation and artifact only."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'Check this' mean argument/evidence validity, writing polish, or novelty/citation fact-checking?

rationale.R1.1: The artifact states a contribution and gap-like positioning that a general check would be expected to assess above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: A general check centrally includes whether the methodological setup, results, and conclusions follow coherently.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation plausibly asks whether the reported percentages, confidence intervals, and training result support the claims made.
rationale.R1.4: The text makes a 'first calibration' contribution claim, so checking may centrally involve novelty relative to prior work.
rationale.R1.6: A broad 'Check this' request commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains potentially strong claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must not execute edits or perform tasks beyond projecting the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported downstream use.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must remain traceable to the given delegation and artifact only.
